intro: Zameioscirpus atacamensis, commonly known as Atacama rush, is a rare and endangered plant species native to the Atacama Desert in Chile. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in treating various conditions such as arthritis, wound healing, and infections. The bioactive constituents of Zameioscirpus atacamensis include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
